source.sourcePath: raw/sources/RA/01_DHF/05_HnX-P1, HnX-PB/05_설계개발 검증 및 유효성 확인/08_Cybersecurity/260303 사용목적 수정(CE 반영)/3. A-CRMR-HNX_Cybersecurity Risk Management Report(Rev.5 03032026).pdf

document.title: Cybersecurity Risk Management Report
standards: Cybersecurity in Medical Devices: Quality System Considerations and Content of Premarket Submissions

sourceSummary.oneLine: Rev. 5 cybersecurity risk management report for HnX-P1 and HnX-PB portable X-ray systems.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Documents cybersecurity risk analysis, evaluation, control, residual risk acceptability, and production/post-production monitoring for the HnX-P1 and HnX-PB.
keyPoints: The report is prepared for sale and usage of HnX-P1 and HnX-PB in accordance with ISO 14971:2019 and TR 24971:2020.
The HnX-P1 and HnX-PB are battery-powered portable X-ray systems intended for generating radiographic images of human anatomy.
HnX-PB is described as a variant model with exterior design changes only; interface, exterior material, internal structure, PCB, and software are the same as HnX-P1.
A total of 59 cybersecurity risks were identified through STRIDE threat modeling.
Initial evaluation identified 13 acceptable risks and 46 unacceptable risks.
After implementation of risk control measures, all 59 residual risks were distributed in a broadly acceptable region.
Cyber threats are to be monitored periodically and cybersecurity risks reviewed once a year.

entities.name: R&D team
entities.evidence: Prepared by Han, Jimin R&D team March 03, 2026

entities.name: Quality Management Representative
entities.evidence: Reviewed by Hur, Namkyu Quality Management Representative March 03, 2026

entities.evidence: Approved by Jeon, Jeu R&D manager March 03, 2026

entities.name: NVD
entities.evidence: After reviewing NIST’s NVD and the CISA KEV Catalog for all off‑the‑shelf and open‑source components in the SBOM, no publicly known vulnerabilities affecting this product were identified.

entities.name: CISA KEV Catalog
entities.evidence: After reviewing NIST’s NVD and the CISA KEV Catalog for all off‑the‑shelf and open‑source components in the SBOM, no publicly known vulnerabilities affecting this product were identified.

concepts.name: Cybersecurity risk management
concepts.evidence: The purpose of this report is making sure for sale and usage of the product to satisfy the safe and expected result considering the intended purpose of HnX-P1 and HnX-PB as preparation of cyber security risk management system in accordance with ISO 14971:2019 , TR 24971:2020.

concepts.name: Risk management process
concepts.evidence: Risk management process needs to be iterative, covering each risk in turn, and returning to earlier steps if risk control measures introduce new hazards or if new information becomes available.

concepts.name: STRIDE Threat modeling
concepts.evidence: A total of 59 risks were identified through STRIDE Threat modeling.

concepts.name: CVSS
concepts.evidence: The Common Vulnerability Scoring System (CVSS) method was applied as an evaluation method for cybersecurity vulnerabilities of the Portable X-ray System (HnX-P1 & HnX-PB).

concepts.name: Residual risk evaluation
concepts.evidence: All identified residual risks evaluated after implementation of the risk control measures, are distributed in a broadly acceptable region.

concepts.name: Firmware and Software Updates
concepts.evidence: Identify the corresponding risks for a total of 8 security items. (Authentication / Authorization / Cryptography / Code, Data, and Execution Integrity / Confidentiality / Event Detection and Logging / Resiliency and Recovery / Firmware and Software Updates)

evidence.claim: The document is Technical Documentation document number A-CRMR-HNX.
evidence.evidenceText: Technical Documentation Document No. A-CRMR-HNX

evidence.claim: The document title is Cybersecurity Risk Management Report.
evidence.evidenceText: Cybersecurity Risk Management Report

evidence.claim: The document revision is Rev. No. 5 and the revision date is March 03, 2026.
evidence.evidenceText: Rev. No. 5 Cybersecurity Risk Management Report Rev. Date March 03, 2026

evidence.claim: The product is a Portable X-ray System with model names HnX-P1 and HnX-PB.
evidence.evidenceText: Product name: Portable X-ray System Model name: HnX-P1, HnX-PB

evidence.claim: The manufacturer is H&abyz Co., Ltd.
evidence.evidenceText: Manufacturer H&abyz Co., Ltd.

evidence.claim: The intended use is generating radiographic images of human anatomy.
evidence.evidenceText: The HnX-P1 (and HnX-PB) is a battery-powered portable X-ray system intended for generating radiographic images of human anatomy.

evidence.claim: HnX-PB is a variant model with only exterior design changes compared to HnX-P1.
evidence.evidenceText: The variant model HnX-PB has only changed the exterior design compared to the main model HnX-P1, and the interface, exterior material, internal structure, PCB, software, etc. are all the same.

evidence.claim: No additional cybersecurity risk management is performed due to the addition of HnX-PB.
evidence.evidenceText: Therefore, no additional cybersecurity risk management is performed due to the addition of the variant model (HnX-PB).

evidence.claim: The report states that the risk management plan has been appropriately implemented, overall residual risk is acceptable, and methods exist to obtain production and post-production information.
evidence.evidenceText: This risk management report ensures the following: - the risk management plan has been appropriately implemented - the overall residual risk is acceptable - appropriate metho ds are in place to obtain relevant production and post production information.

evidence.claim: The report references ISO 14971:2019 and TR 24971:2020 for cybersecurity risk management.
evidence.evidenceText: preparation of cyber security risk management system in accordance with ISO 14971:2019 , TR 24971:2020.

evidence.claim: The device contains software named HnX_P1 and HnX_PB, version Ver 00.12.
evidence.evidenceText: Software name: HnX_P1, HnX_PB (HnX_P1 and HnX_PB, the software for HnX-P1 and HnX-PB, are the same software with different names) • Version: Ver 00.12

evidence.claim: The device uses Bluetooth Low Energy for thumbnail image preview transmission.
evidence.evidenceText: Bluetooth (BLE) Module BoT-nLE521 Standard Bluetooth Low Energy (BLE) ... Function Thumbnail image preview transmission

evidence.claim: The product contains no off-the-shelf software.
evidence.evidenceText: Off-the-shelf: None

evidence.claim: The report states that NIST NVD and CISA KEV Catalog review found no publicly known vulnerabilities affecting the product.
evidence.evidenceText: After reviewing NIST’s NVD and the CISA KEV Catalog for all off‑the‑shelf and open‑source components in the SBOM, no publicly known vulnerabilities affecting this product were identified.

evidence.claim: A total of 59 cybersecurity risks were identified through STRIDE threat modeling.
evidence.evidenceText: A total of 59 risks were identified through STRIDE Threat modeling.

evidence.claim: CVSS version 3.1 was used for cybersecurity risk scoring.
evidence.evidenceText: NVD – CVSS c3 Calculator / CVSS version 3.1

evidence.claim: Initial cybersecurity risk evaluation found 13 acceptable risks and 46 unacceptable risks.
evidence.evidenceText: Green zone, Acceptable Risk 13 Red zone, Unacceptable Risk 46 Total 59

evidence.claim: Risk controls are assigned across eight security items.
evidence.evidenceText: Identify the corresponding risks for a total of 8 security items. (Authentication / Authorization / Cryptography / Code, Data, and Execution Integrity / Confidentiality / Event Detection and Logging / Resiliency and Recovery / Firmware and Software Updates)

evidence.claim: After implementation of risk controls, all 59 residual risks are acceptable and zero are unacceptable.
evidence.evidenceText: Green zone, Acceptable Risk 59 Red zone, Unacceptable Risk 0 Total 59

evidence.claim: No additional risk control measures are needed after residual risk evaluation.
evidence.evidenceText: All identified residual risks evaluated after implementation of the risk control measures, are distributed in a broadly acceptable region. So, there are no further additional risk control measures needed.

evidence.claim: Cyber risks to the product are reviewed once a year and product updates are made if there is a risk.
evidence.evidenceText: cyber risks to the product will be reviewed once a year to update the product if there is a risk.

evidence.claim: Overall residual risks are determined acceptable through cybersecurity testing and threat modeling, labeling, operating method, adverse event reporting, and usability expert review.
evidence.evidenceText: The overall residual risks are determined to be acceptable through cyber security testing and threat modeling, the label, operating method, adverse event reporting and usability expert review.

evidence.claim: Risk management activities for HnX-P1 and HnX-PB were performed and completed according to the risk management plan, excluding production and post-production related activities.
evidence.evidenceText: HnX-P1 and HnX-PB risk management activities according to the risk management plan are performed and completed with appropriately designated personnel exclude the production and post production related activities.

evidence.claim: Post-production cybersecurity activities include annual review of cybersecurity risks and patch release when a threat is believed to exist.
evidence.evidenceText: Review your cybersecurity risks once a year and update them if you believe there is a threat to your cybersecurity.

evidence.claim: Quality manager responsibilities include annual risk analysis for collected production and post-production information.
evidence.evidenceText: Risk analysis for the collected information (Annually)

reviewFlags.reason: Authority or jurisdiction is not explicitly stated in the source text, despite metadata authoritySignal indicating CE/MDR.
Page numbering in the preprocessed text appears inconsistent, for example Page 1 / 2 followed by Page 2 / 3 and later Page 30 / 30.
The text contains OCR or extraction artifacts and typographical errors, including 'metho ds', 'waning', 'Treat modeling', and 'CISC monitoring'.
Risk count wording in CVSS identification appears internally ambiguous: it states 59 risks were scored as High and 13 as Medium, while later tables show 13 acceptable and 46 unacceptable risks.
The document is marked confidential and intended solely for H&abyz Co., Ltd.; wiki publication suitability may require access review.
